props.label: At a Glance · From the Tape Desk

bullets.text: TSMC’s record quarter brought revenue of US$40.20 billion and net-income growth of 77.4%, yet its ADR fell and the Philadelphia semiconductor index dropped about 4.3% on 16 July.
Memory shares found buyers after the initial break. SK Hynix’s ADR, which closed 16 July at $152.31, down 13.69%, rebounded about 5% intraday Friday as Micron gained roughly 4%.
Brent moved toward $87 and WTI toward $82 on Friday, both up about 4%. Strait of Hormuz traffic remained roughly 91% below its pre-war baseline.

items.why: The security-incident and air-quality calls resolve at kickoff. Forecasts indicate that wildfire smoke over the metro should clear before the match.

items.what: US 25% tariff on Brazilian goods takes effect
items.why: Brazil must decide whether to invoke its Reciprocity Law, pursue negotiations or continue cushioning affected producers through domestic credit.

items.what: Next TSM weekly close vs its 16 July $409.74
items.why: A lower weekly close would confirm that the AI-chip derating is outweighing the company’s record demand and investment signals.

items.what: Moonshot’s Kimi K3 full weights due
items.why: A complete open release of a model described as having 2.8 trillion parameters would test the competitive position of US open-model developers.
A settlement at or above $90 would mark a fresh expansion of the Hormuz risk premium as traffic through the strait remains severely reduced.
A rise beyond 3,000 confirmed cases would signal that containment measures had failed to halt the eastern DRC outbreak.

props.meta: Calibrated calls · 2026-07-17 · Cogsworth, Sprockett, Graves, Tinkerton, Foreman · one prior call resolved this edition

open_calls.question: FIFA issues a formal disciplinary charge or sanction against the Argentine federation over the Malvinas banner before the final
open_calls.detail: The disciplinary committee says only that it is assessing the match reports; the 2014 fine for the same slogan came after that tournament, so a charge before Sunday’s kickoff is less likely than one after it.

open_calls.question: The Spain–Argentina final is played without a security incident causing reported injuries or arrests at MetLife
open_calls.detail: The final is a National Special Security Event led by the Secret Service with a protected head of state attending; the base rate for a major incident at such an event is low, though the target profile is high.

open_calls.question: Northeastern New Jersey records an air-quality index of 101 or higher during the final
open_calls.detail: Forecasters expect a late-Saturday front to push the wildfire smoke offshore and leave Sunday dry, but no official Sunday reading has been published and residual haze remains possible.

open_calls.question: Brazil invokes its Reciprocity Law or publishes a concrete non-tariff measure against U.S. firms as the tariff takes effect
open_calls.detail: Brasília has signalled its Reciprocity Law and opened emergency credit, yet has held the legal instrument back; a negotiated path or a delay is at least as likely as a concrete counter by the effective date.

open_calls.question: TSMC names a dated groundbreaking or production milestone for at least one newly announced Arizona fab
open_calls.detail: TSMC committed another $100bn and “four or more” fabs but called the schedule market-dependent and gave no dates; a dated milestone this year is plausible without being the base case.

open_calls.question: Front-month Brent crude settles at or above $90 a barrel
open_calls.detail: Brent jumped toward $87 on renewed U.S.–Iran hostilities and a Strait of Hormuz running about 91% below its baseline, but a settle above $90 needs a further leg that current flows have so far absorbed.

items.what: TSMC raised its 2026 capital-spending range to US$60–64 billion and reported high-performance computing at 66% of revenue, reinforcing the demand signal beneath the semiconductor sell-off.
Brent and WTI rose about 4% on Friday as renewed US–Iran hostilities and the threat of a Red Sea closure lifted supply-risk premiums.
Micron gained about 4% and SK Hynix’s ADR rebounded intraday, while Nvidia slipped roughly 1%, showing a rotation within the chip trade rather than a uniform recovery.
